questions.domain: 領域 3：雲端技術與服務 (Cloud Technology and Services)

question_text.zh: 一家公司计划在 AWS 上部署容器。该公司希望完全控制托管容器的计算资源。哪种 AWS 服务可以满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company plans to deploy containers on AWS. The company wants full control of the compute resources that host the containers. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?

A.zh: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS)
A.en: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS)

B.zh: AWS Fargate
B.en: AWS Fargate

C.zh: Amazon EC2
C.en: Amazon EC2

D.zh: Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS)
D.en: Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS)

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon EKS 是托管式 Kubernetes 服务，可运行容器化应用程序，但计算资源仍由 EC2 节点或 Fargate 提供，服务本身不等于对底层计算资源的完全控制。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon EKS is a managed Kubernetes service suitable for running containerized applications. Although it provides management of Kubernetes, it does not directly provide complete control of the underlying computing resources, which are still supported by EC2 or Fargate.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Fargate 是无服务器容器计算引擎，会抽象底层服务器，因此用户不能完全控制托管容器的计算资源。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This service is a serverless computing engine designed to run containers. It abstracts the management of underlying computing resources, and users cannot directly control the underlying computing resources.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：Amazon EC2 提供对底层计算资源的完全控制。用户可以选择实例类型、操作系统、网络配置等，通过EC2，用户可以手动部署和管理容器化应用，完全满足题目的要求。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: This option provides complete control over the underlying computing resources. Users can choose the instance type, operating system, network configuration, etc. Through EC2, users can manually deploy and manage containerized applications, which fully meets the requirements of the question.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon ECS 是容器编排服务；虽然可配合 EC2 使用，但 ECS 本身不提供题目要求的底层计算资源完全控制。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon ECS is a container orchestration service that supports running containerized applications. While ECS can be used in conjunction with EC2 to provide control of underlying computing resources, ECS itself does not directly provide complete control of computing resources. Therefore this option does not fully meet the question requirements.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (79%) D (21%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：Amazon EC2 提供对底层计算资源的完全控制。用户可以选择实例类型、操作系统、网络配置等，通过EC2，用户可以手动部署和管理容器化应用，完全满足题目的要求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (79%) D (21%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This option provides complete control over the underlying computing resources. Users can choose the instance type, operating system, network configuration, etc. Through EC2, users can manually deploy and manage containerized applications, which fully meets the requirements of the question.

questions.domain: 領域 2：安全與合規 (Security and Compliance)

question_text.zh: 哪种 AWS 服务或功能可让用户创建新的 AWS 账户、将多个账户分组以组织工作流程以及将策略套用至账户组？
question_text.en: Which AWS service or feature allows users to create new AWS accounts, group multiple accounts to organize workflows, and apply policies to groups of accounts?

A.zh: AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)
A.en: AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)

B.zh: AWS Trusted Advisor
B.en: AWS Trusted Advisor

C.zh: AWS CloudFormation
C.en: AWS CloudFormation

D.zh: AWS Organizations
D.en: AWS Organizations

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：IAM 管理单个 AWS 账户中的用户、角色和权限，不负责创建账户、组织账户或向账户组应用策略。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: IAM is primarily used to manage permissions for users, groups, and roles, rather than creating or organizing multiple AWS accounts.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Trusted Advisor 提供成本、安全性、性能和可靠性建议，不用于创建和组织多个 AWS 账户。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Trusted Advisor is an optimization tool used to examine accounts for security, cost efficiency, etc., but does not involve account creation or organizational functionality.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS CloudFormation 用于以模板预置和管理 AWS 资源，不负责创建账户组或应用服务控制策略。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: CloudFormation is used to automate the deployment and management of resources rather than managing multiple accounts or applying policies.

D.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS Organizations 允许用户创建和分组 AWS 账户，并将服务控制策略套用到组织单位，这直接满足了要求。
D.en: Correct. Technical reason: This option allows users to create and group AWS accounts and apply service control policies to organizational units, which directly meets the requirement.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：D (83%) A (17%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS Organizations 允许用户创建和分组 AWS 账户，并将服务控制策略套用到组织单位，这直接满足了要求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (83%) A (17%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This option allows users to create and group AWS accounts and apply service control policies to organizational units, which directly meets the requirement.

questions.domain: 領域 3：雲端技術與服務 (Cloud Technology and Services)

question_text.zh: 一家公司希望使用业界标准文件系统协议在 Amazon S3 中为其现有本地应用程序存储和检索文件。哪种 AWS 服务可以满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company wants to store and retrieve files in Amazon S3 for its existing on-premises applications by using industry-standard file system protocols. Which AWS service will meet these requirements?

A.zh: AWS DataSync
A.en: AWS DataSync

B.zh: AWS Snowball Edge
B.en: AWS Snowball Edge

C.zh: Amazon S3 File Gateway
C.en: Amazon S3 File Gateway

D.zh: AWS Transfer Family
D.en: AWS Transfer Family

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS DataSync 用于自动化数据传输，不会为本地应用程序提供持续的 NFS 或 SMB 文件系统接口。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This service is mainly used for automated data migration. It is suitable for migrating large amounts of data from local storage to AWS services (such as S3, EFS, etc.), but it does not provide file system protocol support.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Snowball Edge 主要用于离线大规模数据迁移和边缘计算，不适合作为持续访问 Amazon S3 的标准文件系统接口。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This service is a physical device used for offline data migration or edge computing and is not suitable for real-time file storage and retrieval needs.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：Amazon S3 File Gateway 是 AWS Storage Gateway 的型号，支持业界标准文件系统协议（例如 NFS 和 SMB），并将文件存储在 Amazon S3 中，完全符合问题要求。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: This service is a model of AWS Storage Gateway that supports industry-standard file system protocols (such as NFS and SMB) and stores files in Amazon S3, which fully meets the question requirements.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Transfer Family 提供 SFTP、FTPS 和 FTP 文件传输，不提供 NFS 或 SMB 文件系统协议接口。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This service is designed to support file transfers with SFTP, FTPS, and FTP protocols, rather than file system protocols (such as NFS or SMB).

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (82%) D (18%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：Amazon S3 File Gateway 是 AWS Storage Gateway 的型号，支持业界标准文件系统协议（例如 NFS 和 SMB），并将文件存储在 Amazon S3 中，完全符合问题要求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (82%) D (18%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This service is a model of AWS Storage Gateway that supports industry-standard file system protocols (such as NFS and SMB) and stores files in Amazon S3, which fully meets the question requirements.

questions.domain: 領域 2：安全與合規 (Security and Compliance)

question_text.zh: 一家公司想要阻止 SQL 注入攻击。公司应使用哪种 AWS 服务或功能来满足此要求？
question_text.en: A company wants to block SQL injection attacks. Which AWS service or feature should the company use to meet this requirement?

A.zh: AWS WAF
A.en: AWS WAF

B.zh: 网络 ACL
B.en: Network ACLs

C.zh: 安全组
C.en: Security groups

D.zh: AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)
D.en: AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)

A.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS WAF 可通过 Web ACL 规则检查 HTTP(S) 请求并阻止 SQL 注入和跨站脚本等常见 Web 攻击。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: Web Application Firewall) is a service specifically designed to protect web applications from exploit attacks such as SQL injection and cross-site scripting attacks. It is allowed to use custom rules to filter traffic.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：网络 ACL 在子网层根据协议、端口和地址控制流量，无法检查应用层请求内容以识别 SQL 注入。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Network ACL (Access Control List) is a tool used to control inbound and outbound traffic at the subnet level, but it cannot detect or prevent SQL injection and other malicious attacks. divide harm

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：安全组在资源层控制入站和出站连接，不会分析 SQL 查询或 HTTP 请求内容。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The server is a tool used to control inbound and outbound traffic at the instance level. It is mainly based on IP address and port number and cannot handle malicious attacks such as SQL injection.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：ACM 用于管理 SSL/TLS 凭证以增强数据传输，但它不提供针对 SQL 注入攻击的保护。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: ACM is used to manage SSL/TLS certificates to enhance data transmission, but it does not provide protection against SQL injection attacks.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：A (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS WAF 可通过 Web ACL 规则检查 HTTP(S) 请求并阻止 SQL 注入和跨站脚本等常见 Web 攻击。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Web Application Firewall) is a service specifically designed to protect web applications from exploit attacks such as SQL injection and cross-site scripting attacks. It is allowed to use custom rules to filter traffic.

questions.domain: 領域 3：雲端技術與服務 (Cloud Technology and Services)

question_text.zh: 一家公司需要一个统一的工具来提供与 AWS 服务交互的一致方法。哪种 AWS 服务或工具可以满足此要求？
question_text.en: A company wants a unified tool to provide a consistent method to interact with AWS services. Which AWS service or tool will meet this requirement?

A.zh: AWS CLI
A.en: AWS CLI

B.zh: Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS)
B.en: Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS)

C.zh: AWS Cloud9
C.en: AWS Cloud9

D.zh: AWS Virtual Private Network (AWS VPN)
D.en: AWS Virtual Private Network (AWS VPN)

A.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS CLI 提供统一的命令行界面，可用一致方式调用各项 AWS 服务，并适合脚本和自动化任务。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: Command Line Interface) is a unified tool that provides a consistent method to interact with AWS services. It supports command line operations and is suitable for automated and scripted tasks.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon ECS 用于运行和管理容器化应用程序，不是用于统一调用 AWS 服务的交互工具。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon ECS is a container orchestration service for running and managing containerized applications, rather than a unified interactive tool.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Cloud9 是一个基于云的集成开发环境 (IDE)，主要用于开发和调试器码，而不是统一的交互工具。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This service is a cloud-based integrated development environment (IDE) mainly used for developing and debugging code, rather than a unified interactive tool.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS VPN 用于建立安全网络连接，不提供管理 AWS 服务的统一命令界面。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: AWS VPN is used to establish a secure network connection and has nothing to do with interacting with AWS services.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：A (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS CLI 提供统一的命令行界面，可用一致方式调用各项 AWS 服务，并适合脚本和自动化任务。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Command Line Interface) is a unified tool that provides a consistent method to interact with AWS services. It supports command line operations and is suitable for automated and scripted tasks.

questions.domain: 領域 4：計費、定價與支援 (Billing, Pricing, and Support)

question_text.zh: 公司需要评估其 AWS 环境并提供五个类别的最佳实践建议：成本、性能、服务限制、容错和安全性。公司可以使用哪种 AWS 服务来满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company needs to evaluate its AWS environment and provide best practice recommendations in five categories: cost, performance, service limits, fault tolerance and security. Which AWS service can the company use to meet these requirements?

A.zh: AWS Shield
A.en: AWS Shield

B.zh: AWS WAF
B.en: AWS WAF

C.zh: AWS Trusted Advisor
C.en: AWS Trusted Advisor

D.zh: AWS Service Catalog
D.en: AWS Service Catalog

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Shield 是一项专注于保护应用程序免受 DDoS 攻击的服务。它不提供环境评估或最佳实践建议，
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This service is a service focused on protecting applications from DDoS attacks. It does not provide environmental assessments or best practice recommendations,

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS WAF 是一种 Web 应用程序防火墙服务，可保护 Web 应用程序免受常见攻击。它也不涉及环境评估或最佳实践建议，
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This service is a web application firewall service that protects web applications from common attacks. It also does not involve environmental assessment or best practice recommendations,

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS Trusted Advisor 是一项旨在评估 AWS 环境并提供最佳实践建议的服务，包括成本优化、性能、服务限制、容错和安全性。这正是问题所要求的功能。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: This service is a service designed to evaluate AWS environments and provide best practice recommendations, including cost optimization, performance, service limits, fault tolerance, and security. This is exactly the function required by the question.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Service Catalog 用于管理和部署预先定义的云资源集合。它不提供环境评估或最佳实践建议，
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This service is used to manage and deploy a predefined collection of cloud resources. It does not provide environmental assessments or best practice recommendations,

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS Trusted Advisor 是一项旨在评估 AWS 环境并提供最佳实践建议的服务，包括成本优化、性能、服务限制、容错和安全性。这正是问题所要求的功能。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This service is a service designed to evaluate AWS environments and provide best practice recommendations, including cost optimization, performance, service limits, fault tolerance, and security. This is exactly the function required by the question.

questions.domain: 領域 1：雲端概念 (Cloud Concepts)

question_text.zh: AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) 中的哪个观点包含配置管理和修补程序管理功能？
question_text.en: Which perspective in the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) includes capabilities for configuration management and patch management?

A.zh: 平台视角
A.en: Platform

B.zh: 运营视角
B.en: Operations

C.zh: 安全视角
C.en: Security

D.zh: 治理视角
D.en: Governance

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：平台视角侧重应用程序、数据和基础设施平台的设计与构建；配置管理和补丁管理属于运营视角的能力。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The platform perspective focuses on the design and construction of technology platforms, including the architecture of applications, data, and infrastructure. Although the platform perspective involves technology, it is not directly responsible for configuration management and patch management.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：运营：运营观点着重于日常运营和管理任务，包括监控、事件响应、配置管理、修补程序管理等。这与问题中的「配置管理」和「修补程序管理」直接相关。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: The operations perspective focuses on daily operations and management tasks, including monitoring, incident response, configuration management, patch management, etc. This is directly related to "configuration management" and "patch management" in the question.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：性：安全观点着重于保护数据、应用程序和基础设施的能力，包括身份管理、访问控制和数据保护。虽然安全性可能涉及修补程序管理，但它主要不是配置管理的角度。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The security perspective focuses on the ability to protect data, applications, and infrastructure, including identity management, access control, and data protection. While security may involve patch management, it is not primarily a configuration management perspective.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：的角度涉及确保组织遵守法规、策略和标准，包括财务管理和风险管理。它与配置管理和修补程序管理没有直接关系。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The governance perspective is concerned with ensuring that the organization complies with regulations, policies, and standards, including financial management and risk management. It is not directly related to configuration management and patch management.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：B (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：运营：运营观点着重于日常运营和管理任务，包括监控、事件响应、配置管理、修补程序管理等。这与问题中的「配置管理」和「修补程序管理」直接相关。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: The operations perspective focuses on daily operations and management tasks, including monitoring, incident response, configuration management, patch management, etc. This is directly related to "configuration management" and "patch management" in the question.

questions.domain: 領域 4：計費、定價與支援 (Billing, Pricing, and Support)

question_text.zh: 公司拥有稳定、可预测且不间断的计算工作负载。哪些 Amazon EC2 实例购买选项最经济且高效地满足这些要求？ （选择两项。）
question_text.en: A company has a compute workload that is steady, predictable, and uninterruptible. Which Amazon EC2 instance purchasing options meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively? (Choose two.)

A.zh: 按需型实例
A.en: On-Demand Instances

B.zh: 预留实例
B.en: Reserved Instances

C.zh: 竞价型实例
C.en: Spot Instances

D.zh: 节省计划
D.en: Savings Plans

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：（按需运行实例）按需运行实例适用于不需要长期承诺的短期、不可预测的工作负载。虽然灵活，但成本较高，并不是最具成本效益的选择。不符合「性价比」的要求。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: (On-demand execution instances) On-demand execution instances are suitable for short-term, unpredictable workloads that do not require long-term commitments. Although flexible, the cost is higher and not the most cost-effective option. Does not meet the requirement of "cost-effectiveness".

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：（预留运行实例）预留实例适用于稳定且可预测的工作负载，用户可以通过长期承诺（1年或3年）获得显著的成本节省。满足题中「稳定、可预测、不可中断」的要求。最具成本效益的选择之一。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: (Reserved Execution Instances) Reserved Instances are suitable for stable and predictable workloads, and users can obtain significant cost savings through long-term commitments (1 or 3 years). It meets the requirements of "stable, predictable, and uninterruptible" in the question. One of the most cost-effective options.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：（竞价型实例）Spot 运行实例价格便宜，但可能会因容量需求而中断。不适合「不可中断」的工作负载。不符合「不间断」的要求。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: (Spot Instances) Spot execution instances are cheap but may be interrupted due to capacity needs. Not suitable for "non-interruptible" workloads. Does not meet the "uninterruptible" requirement.

D.zh: 正確。技术原因：(Savings Plan) Savings Plan 为稳定的工作负载提供灵活的定价选项，让用户可以通过长期承诺节省成本。满足题中「稳定、可预测、不可中断」的要求。最具成本效益的选择之一。
D.en: Correct. Technical reason: (Savings Plan) The Savings Plan offers flexible pricing options for stable workloads, allowing users to gain cost savings through a long-term commitment. It meets the requirements of "stable, predictable, and uninterruptible" in the question. One of the most cost-effective options.

E.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：（专用主机）专用主机提供物理隔离，适合满足合规性或授权要求的工作负载。成本较高，并不是最具成本效益的选择。不符合「性价比」的要求。
E.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: (Dedicated Hosts) Dedicated hosts provide physical isolation and are suitable for workloads that meet compliance or licensing requirements. The cost is higher and not the most cost-effective option. Does not meet the requirement of "cost-effectiveness".

answer_text.zh: B. 预留实例；D. 节省计划
answer_text.en: B. Reserved Instances; D. Savings Plans

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：BD (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：（预留运行实例）预留实例适用于稳定且可预测的工作负载，用户可以通过长期承诺（1年或3年）获得显著的成本节省。满足题中「稳定、可预测、不可中断」的要求。最具成本效益的选择之一。；(Savings Plan) Savings Plan 为稳定的工作负载提供灵活的定价选项，让用户可以通过长期承诺节省成本。满足题中「稳定、可预测、不可中断」的要求。最具成本效益的选择之一。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: BD (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: (Reserved Execution Instances) Reserved Instances are suitable for stable and predictable workloads, and users can obtain significant cost savings through long-term commitments (1 or 3 years). It meets the requirements of "stable, predictable, and uninterruptible" in the question. One of the most cost-effective options.; (Savings Plan) The Savings Plan offers flexible pricing options for stable workloads, allowing users to gain cost savings through a long-term commitment. It meets the requirements of "stable, predictable, and uninterruptible" in the question. One of the most cost-effective options.

questions.domain: 領域 4：計費、定價與支援 (Billing, Pricing, and Support)

question_text.zh: 对于每年运行一次、持续 24 小时的不间断工作负载，哪种 Amazon EC2 定价模型最具成本效益？
question_text.en: Which Amazon EC2 pricing model is the MOST cost efficient for an uninterruptible workload that runs once a year for 24 hours?

A.zh: 按需型实例
A.en: On-Demand Instances

B.zh: 预留实例
B.en: Reserved Instances

C.zh: 竞价型实例
C.en: Spot Instances

D.zh: Dedicated Instances
D.en: Dedicated Instances

A.zh: 正確。技术原因：非常适合没有长期承诺的短期、不可预测的工作负载。对于每年运行一次的工作负载，按需型实例提供了灵活性和无需预付费用的优势。这是正确的选择，因为它最适合此类不间断的短期工作负载。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: Ideal for short-term, unpredictable workloads without long-term commitments. For workloads that run once a year, On-Demand instances offer flexibility and the benefit of no upfront costs. The right option as it is best suited for this type of non-stop, short-term workload.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：适合长期稳定的工作负载，需事先承诺使用期间（1年或3年）。虽然成本较低，但它不适合每年仅运行一次的工作负载，因为它需要预付款或时间投入。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: It is suitable for long-term stable workloads and requires an advance commitment for a usage period (1 year or 3 years). While lower cost, it is not suitable for workloads that only run once a year because it requires upfront payment or time commitment.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：非常适合能够以最低成本容忍中断的弹性工作负载。不适合不间断工作负载，因为单一 Spot 运行可能会中断。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Ideal for flexible workloads that can tolerate outages at minimal cost. Not suitable for non-stop workloads as individual Spot executions may be interrupted.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：为需要满足合规性或特殊硬件需求的工作负载提供物理隔离的服务器。成本较高，不适合短期运转的工作负载。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option provides physically isolated servers for workloads that need to meet compliance or special hardware requirements. Higher cost and not suitable for short-running workloads.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：A (90%) 10%。热门讨论与考点摘要：非常适合没有长期承诺的短期、不可预测的工作负载。对于每年运行一次的工作负载，按需型实例提供了灵活性和无需预付费用的优势。这是正确的选择，因为它最适合此类不间断的短期工作负载。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (90%) 10%. Popular discussion and key point summary: Ideal for short-term, unpredictable workloads without long-term commitments. For workloads that run once a year, On-Demand instances offer flexibility and the benefit of no upfront costs. The right option as it is best suited for this type of non-stop, short-term workload.

questions.domain: 領域 1：雲端概念 (Cloud Concepts)

question_text.zh: 在 AWS 责任共担模式下，哪个选项是 AWS 与客户共同承担的责任？
question_text.en: Which option is a shared responsibility between AWS and its customers under the AWS shared responsibility model?

A.zh: Amazon EC2 实例操作系统的配置
A.en: Configuration of Amazon EC2 instance operating systems

B.zh: 应用程序文件系统服务器端加密
B.en: Application file system server-side encryption

C.zh: 修补程序管理
C.en: Patch management

D.zh: 物理基础设施的安全
D.en: Security of the physical infrastructure

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：（Amazon EC2 实例操作系统配置）客户负责设置和管理 EC2 实例操作系统，包括安装软件、设置权限等。这是客户的责任，而不是共同责任。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: (Amazon EC2 instance operating system configuration) Customers are responsible for configuring and managing the EC2 instance operating system, including installing software, setting permissions, etc. This is the client's responsibility and is not a shared responsibility.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：客户决定是否为应用程序文件系统启用服务器端加密并管理相关配置；这不是题目所指最典型的双方共享控制。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: (Application file system server-side encryption) Server-side encryption is a service function provided by AWS, and AWS is responsible for implementing and maintaining the encryption mechanism. Customers are responsible for choosing whether to enable encryption and managing associated encryption keys. This is a joint responsibility of the customer and AWS, but it does not fully meet the core definition of "shared responsibility" in the question.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：补丁管理属于共享控制：AWS 修补底层基础设施，客户负责修补其来宾操作系统和应用程序。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: (Patch Management) Patch management is part of shared responsibility. AWS manages patches for its infrastructure (such as hosts and network devices), while customers manage patches for their operating systems and applications.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：（物理基础设施的安全）物理基础设施的安全完全由 AWS 负责，无需客户参与。这不是共同责任。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: (Security of physical infrastructure) The security of physical infrastructure is entirely the responsibility of AWS, without customer involvement. This is not shared responsibility.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：补丁管理属于共享控制：AWS 修补底层基础设施，客户负责修补其来宾操作系统和应用程序。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: (Patch Management) Patch management is part of shared responsibility. AWS manages patches for its infrastructure (such as hosts and network devices), while customers manage patches for their operating systems and applications.

questions.domain: 領域 4：計費、定價與支援 (Billing, Pricing, and Support)

question_text.zh: 一家公司希望将其本地工作负载迁移到 AWS 云。该公司希望将费用分摊给不同部门的工作负载。哪些 AWS 服务或功能可以满足这些要求？ （选择两项。）
question_text.en: A company wants to migrate its on-premises workloads to the AWS Cloud. The company wants to separate workloads for chargeback to different departments. Which AWS services or features will meet these requirements? (Choose two.)

A.zh: 置放群组
A.en: Placement groups

B.zh: 合并账单
B.en: Consolidated billing

C.zh: 边缘位置
C.en: Edge locations

D.zh: AWS Config
D.en: AWS Config

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：该选项 用于优化网络性能或减少单一运行的延迟，与成本分配或工作负载隔离无关。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: These choices are used to optimize network performance or reduce latency of individual executions, regardless of cost allocation or workload isolation.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：合并计费允许多个AWS 账户共享一个支付账户，并提供详细的成本分配报告，适合部门之间的成本分配，
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: This option allows multiple AWS accounts to share a payment account and provides detailed cost allocation reports, suitable for cost allocation between departments,

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：边缘网站用于内容分发（例如 Amazon CloudFront），与成本分摊或工作负载隔离无关。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: These choices are used for content distribution (such as Amazon CloudFront) and have nothing to do with cost sharing or workload isolation.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Config 用于记录资源配置并支持审计与合规检查，不用于按部门划分账单或隔离工作负载成本。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This service is used for auditing and compliance checking of resource configurations, regardless of cost allocation or workload isolation,

E.zh: 正確。技术原因：使用多个 AWS 账户可以通过集成计费实现工作负载隔离和成本分摊。
E.en: Correct. Technical reason: Using multiple AWS accounts enables workload isolation and cost sharing through Consolidated billing.

answer_text.zh: B. 合并账单；E. 多个 AWS 账户
answer_text.en: B. Consolidated billing; E. Multiple AWS accounts

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：BE (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：合并计费允许多个AWS 账户共享一个支付账户，并提供详细的成本分配报告，适合部门之间的成本分配，；使用多个 AWS 账户可以通过集成计费实现工作负载隔离和成本分摊。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: BE (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This option allows multiple AWS accounts to share a payment account and provides detailed cost allocation reports, suitable for cost allocation between departments,; Using multiple AWS accounts enables workload isolation and cost sharing through Consolidated billing.

questions.domain: 領域 1：雲端概念 (Cloud Concepts)

question_text.zh: 根据 AWS 责任共担模式，哪一项任务是 AWS 的责任？
question_text.en: Which task is a responsibility of AWS, according to the AWS shared responsibility model?

A.zh: 为 Amazon S3 中存储的对象启用客户端加密。
A.en: Enable client-side encryption for objects that are stored in Amazon S3.

B.zh: 配置 IAM 安全策略以遵循最小权限原则。
B.en: Configure IAM security policies to comply with the principle of least privilege.

C.zh: 修补 Amazon EC2 实例上的来宾操作系统。
C.en: Patch the guest operating system on an Amazon EC2 instance.

D.zh: 将更新套用到 Nitro Hypervisor。
D.en: Apply updates to the Nitro Hypervisor.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：根据责任共担模式，客户负责保护自己的数据，包括决定并实施 Amazon S3 对象的客户端加密。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Under the shared responsibility model, the customer is responsible for the encryption and protection of data, including enabling client-side encryption. This is the customer's responsibility, not AWS's.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：客户有责任设置 IAM 安全性原则以满足最小权限原则。 AWS提供工具（如IAM）供客户使用，但具体配置由客户负责。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: It is the customer's responsibility to configure IAM security policies to comply with the principle of least privilege. AWS provides tools (such as IAM) for customers to use, but the specific configuration is the responsibility of the customer.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：客户有责任修补 EC2 实例上的客户操作系统。 AWS 负责底层硬件和虚拟化层的维护，但操作系统的管理是客户的责任。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Error: It is the customer's responsibility to patch the customer operating system on the EC2 instance. AWS is responsible for the maintenance of the underlying hardware and virtualization layer, but the management of the operating system is the responsibility of the customer.

D.zh: 正確。技术原因：Nitro Hypervisor 是 AWS 管理的虚拟化层的一部分。根据责任共担模式，AWS 负责维护和更新底层基础设施，包括Nitro Hypervisor。
D.en: Correct. Technical reason: Nitro Hypervisor is part of the virtualization layer managed by AWS. According to the shared responsibility model, AWS is responsible for maintaining and updating the underlying infrastructure, including Nitro Hypervisor.

answer_text.zh: D. 将更新套用到 Nitro Hypervisor。
answer_text.en: D. Apply updates to the Nitro Hypervisor.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：D (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：Nitro Hypervisor 是 AWS 管理的虚拟化层的一部分。根据责任共担模式，AWS 负责维护和更新底层基础设施，包括Nitro Hypervisor。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Nitro Hypervisor is part of the virtualization layer managed by AWS. According to the shared responsibility model, AWS is responsible for maintaining and updating the underlying infrastructure, including Nitro Hypervisor.

questions.domain: 領域 1：雲端概念 (Cloud Concepts)

question_text.zh: 使用 AWS 进行云计算的好处是什么？
question_text.en: Which option is a benefit of using AWS for cloud computing?

A.zh: 将可变费用换成固定费用
A.en: Trade variable expense for fixed expense

B.zh: 按使用量付费定价
B.en: Pay-as-you-go pricing

C.zh: 速度与敏捷性降低
C.en: Decreased speed and agility

D.zh: 花钱运行和维护数据中心
D.en: Spending money running and maintaining data centers

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS 的按需付费模式是将固定费用转为可变费用，而不是将可变费用转为固定费用，因此不符合题目要求。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Convert variable charges to fixed charges Error: AWS's pricing model is pay-as-you-go, allowing users to pay based on actual usage, rather than converting variable charges to fixed charges.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：按使用量付费是 AWS 云计算的核心优势之一。客户只需为实际使用的资源付费，无需预付费用或作出长期承诺。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: This service is One of the core advantages of AWS is pay-as-you-go pricing. Users only pay for the resources they actually use, without upfront payment or long-term commitment.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS 云计算能够提高速度和敏捷性，而不是降低速度和敏捷性，因此不符合题目要求。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Reducing Barduity and Agility Error: AWS's cloud computing services actually increase speed and agility, rather than decreasing them.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：使用 AWS 可以减少运行和维护数据中心的支出，因此该选项不符合题目要求。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Flowers and data center maintenance errors: Using AWS can reduce the user's expenditure on running and maintaining the data center, rather than increasing this expenditure.

answer_text.zh: B. 按使用量付费定价

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：B (87%) 13%。热门讨论与考点摘要：按使用量付费是 AWS 云计算的核心优势之一。客户只需为实际使用的资源付费，无需预付费用或作出长期承诺。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (87%) 13%. Popular discussion and key point summary: This service is One of the core advantages of AWS is pay-as-you-go pricing. Users only pay for the resources they actually use, without upfront payment or long-term commitment.

questions.domain: 領域 1：雲端概念 (Cloud Concepts)

question_text.zh: 哪个选项是 AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) 业务视角功能？
question_text.en: Which option is an AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) business perspective capability?

A.zh: 文化演变
A.en: Culture evolution

B.zh: 事件管理
B.en: Event management

C.zh: 数据货币化
C.en: Data monetization

D.zh: 平台架构
D.en: Platform architecture

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：（文化进化）：错误。文化进化是从人的角度来看，而不是从商业的角度来看。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: (Cultural Evolution): Error. Cultural evolution is a people perspective, not a business perspective.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：（事件管理）：错误。事件管理属于运营角度，与业务角度无关。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: (Event Management): Error. Incident management belongs to the Operations Perspective and has nothing to do with the business perspective.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：（数据货币化）：数据货币化是商业视角的一部分，旨在通过数据驱动的决策和创新来实现商业价值。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: (Data Monetization): Correct. Data monetization is part of a business perspective that aims to realize business value through data-driven decisions and innovation.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：（平台架构）：错误。平台架构属于平台视角，与业务视角无关。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: (Platform Architecture): Error. Platform architecture belongs to the Platform Perspective and has nothing to do with the business perspective.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (88%) 12%。热门讨论与考点摘要：（数据货币化）：数据货币化是商业视角的一部分，旨在通过数据驱动的决策和创新来实现商业价值。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (88%) 12%. Popular discussion and key point summary: (Data Monetization): Correct. Data monetization is part of a business perspective that aims to realize business value through data-driven decisions and innovation.

questions.domain: 領域 4：計費、定價與支援 (Billing, Pricing, and Support)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在评估其 AWS 商业支持计划，以确定该计划是否仍满足公司的需求。该公司正在考虑转向 AWS Enterprise Support。公司将通过 AWS Enterprise Support 获得哪些额外好处？
question_text.en: A company is assessing its AWS Business Support plan to determine if the plan still meets the company’s needs. The company is considering switching to AWS Enterprise Support. Which additional benefit will the company receive with AWS Enterprise Support?

A.zh: 全套 AWS Trusted Advisor 检查
A.en: A full set of AWS Trusted Advisor checks

B.zh: 每周 7 天、每天 24 小时通过电话、电子邮件和聊天方式联系云支持工程师
B.en: Phone, email, and chat access to cloud support engineers 24 hours a day, 7 days a week

C.zh: 指定技术客户经理 (TAM) 协助监控和优化
C.en: A designated technical account manager (TAM) to assist in monitoring and optimization

D.zh: 公司应用程序的咨询审查和架构指南
D.en: A consultative review and architecture guidance for the company’s applications

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS 商业支持也提供完整的 AWS Trusted Advisor 检查，因此这不是企业支持独有的优势。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Full set of AWS Trusted Advisor checks - Error: The AWS Business Support plan does not provide a full set of AWS Trusted Advisor checks, so this is not an additional benefit of AWS Enterprise Support.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：此功能包含在 AWS 商业支持计划中，因此它不是 AWS 企业支持的额外优势。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week access to cloud support engineers via phone, email, and chat - Error: This feature is included in the AWS Business Support plan, so it is not an additional benefit of AWS Enterprise Support.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS 企业支持计划提供指定技术客户经理 (TAM)，这是该计划的主要福利之一。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: Designated Technical Account Manager (TAM) to assist with monitoring and optimization - The AWS Enterprise Support plan provides a designated Technical Account Manager (TAM), which is one of the key perks of the plan.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：企业应用程序的咨询审查和架构指南 - 错误：虽然 AWS Enterprise Support 确实提供了架构指南，但这并不是一项独特的福利，因为 AWS Business Support 也提供了类似的指南。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Consultative Review and Architectural Guidance for Corporate Applications - Error: While AWS Enterprise Support does provide architectural guidance, this is not a unique perk as AWS Business Support also provides similar guidance.

answer_text.zh: C. 指定技术客户经理 (TAM) 协助监控和优化
answer_text.en: C. A designated technical account manager (TAM) to assist in monitoring and optimization

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (54%) D (46%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS 企业支持计划提供指定技术客户经理 (TAM)，这是该计划的主要福利之一。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (54%) D (46%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Designated Technical Account Manager (TAM) to assist with monitoring and optimization - The AWS Enterprise Support plan provides a designated Technical Account Manager (TAM), which is one of the key perks of the plan.

questions.domain: 領域 4：計費、定價與支援 (Billing, Pricing, and Support)

question_text.zh: 如果容量暂时无法使用，哪一种定价模型会中断正在运行的 Amazon EC2 实例？
question_text.en: Which pricing model will interrupt a running Amazon EC2 instance if capacity becomes temporarily unavailable?

A.zh: 按需型实例
A.en: On-Demand Instances

B.zh: 标准预留实例
B.en: Standard Reserved Instances

C.zh: 竞价型实例
C.en: Spot Instances

D.zh: 可转换预留实例
D.en: Convertible Reserved Instances

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：按需运行实例：按需运行实例不会因容量问题而中断。用户按小时或秒计费，可以随时启动或停止实例，适合短期或不可预测的工作负载。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: On-demand execution instances: On-demand execution instances will not be interrupted due to capacity issues. Users are billed by the hour or second, and can start or stop executing individuals at any time, which is suitable for short-term or unpredictable workloads.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：标准预留运行实例：标准预留运行实例是用户提前付费换取长期使用折扣的系统。容量是预留的，不会因为容量问题而中断。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Standard reserved execution instance: Standard reserved execution instance is a system where users pay in advance in exchange for discounts for long-term use. The capacity is reserved and will not be interrupted due to capacity issues.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：竞价型实例：竞价型实例以较低的价格运行，但可能会因容量不足或价格超过用户设置的上限而中断。这是 竞价型实例的核心功能。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: Spot Instance: Spot Instance runs at a low price, but may be interrupted due to insufficient capacity or price exceeding the upper limit set by the user. This is the core feature of the Spot Instance.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：可转换预留运行实例：可转换预留运行实例允许用户在预留期间变更运行实例类型，但不会因容量问题而中断。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Convertible reserved execution instances: Convertible reserved execution instances allow users to change execution instance types during the reservation period, but will not be interrupted due to capacity issues.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：竞价型实例：竞价型实例以较低的价格运行，但可能会因容量不足或价格超过用户设置的上限而中断。这是 竞价型实例的核心功能。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Spot Instance: Spot Instance runs at a low price, but may be interrupted due to insufficient capacity or price exceeding the upper limit set by the user. This is the core feature of the Spot Instance.

questions.domain: 領域 2：安全與合規 (Security and Compliance)

question_text.zh: AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) 安全性视角功能包含哪些选项？ （选择两项。）
question_text.en: Which options are AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) security perspective capabilities? (Choose two.)

A.zh: 可观察性
A.en: Observability

B.zh: 事件与问题管理
B.en: Incident and problem management

C.zh: 事件响应
C.en: Incident response

D.zh: 基础设施保护
D.en: Infrastructure protection

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：可观察性通常属于操作角度，而非安全角度。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Error. Observability generally belongs to the Operations Perspective, not the security perspective.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：事件和问题管理属于运营视角（Operations Perspective），而非安全视角。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Error. Incident and problem management belongs to the operations perspective (Operations Perspective), not the security perspective.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：事件响应是安全视角的重要能力之一，用于处理安全事件并减少其影响。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: Event response: Correct. Incident response is one of the important capabilities of the security perspective, used to handle security incidents and reduce their impact.

D.zh: 正確。技术原因：基础设施防护是安全视野的核心能力之一，用于保障云资源的安全。
D.en: Correct. Technical reason: Correct. Infrastructure protection is one of the core capabilities of the security perspective and is used to ensure the security of cloud resources.

E.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：可用性和连续性一般属于可靠性视角（Reliability Perspective），而非安全视角。
E.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Error. Availability and continuity generally belong to the reliability perspective (Reliability Perspective), rather than the security perspective.

answer_text.zh: C. 事件响应；D. 基础设施保护
answer_text.en: C. Incident response; D. Infrastructure protection

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：CD (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：事件响应是安全视角的重要能力之一，用于处理安全事件并减少其影响。；基础设施防护是安全视野的核心能力之一，用于保障云资源的安全。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: CD (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Event response: Correct. Incident response is one of the important capabilities of the security perspective, used to handle security incidents and reduce their impact.; Correct. Infrastructure protection is one of the core capabilities of the security perspective and is used to ensure the security of cloud resources.

questions.domain: 領域 4：計費、定價與支援 (Billing, Pricing, and Support)

question_text.zh: 一家公司希望在 Amazon EC2 实例上运行其工作负载超过 1 年。此工作负载将持续运行。与按需型实例的每小时费率相比，哪个选项提供每小时费率折扣？
question_text.en: A company wants to run its workload on Amazon EC2 instances for more than 1 year. This workload will run continuously. Which option offers a discounted hourly rate compared to the hourly rate of On-Demand Instances?

A.zh: AWS Graviton 处理器
A.en: AWS Graviton processor

B.zh: 专用主机
B.en: Dedicated Hosts

C.zh: EC2 实例节省计划
C.en: EC2 Instance Savings Plans

D.zh: Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling 实例
D.en: Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling instances

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Graviton 处理器是基于 ARM 架构的处理器，可提供更高的性能和更低的成本，但它独立于定价模型。错误。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The AWS Graviton processor is an ARM architecture-based processor that offers higher performance and lower cost, but it is independent of the pricing model. mistake.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：专用主机为客户提供物理服务器的专用使用权，主要满足合规或许可需求，通常不是降低持续工作负载小时费率的最佳方案。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option provide exclusive use rights to physical servers, which is suitable for scenarios that meet compliance or licensing requirements, but the cost is usually higher and no discounts are provided. mistake.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：EC2 实例节省计划通过承诺一定期限的计算使用量，为长期持续运行的 EC2 工作负载提供相对于按需型实例的折扣。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: This option allow users to receive discounts by committing to use specific types of EC2 instances, suitable for long-running workloads. correct.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Auto Scaling 是一项动态调整实例数量以优化成本的服务，但与定价折扣无关。错误。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Auto Scaling is a service that dynamically adjusts the number of instances to optimize costs, but it has nothing to do with pricing discounts. mistake.

answer_text.zh: C. EC2 实例节省计划

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：EC2 实例节省计划通过承诺一定期限的计算使用量，为长期持续运行的 EC2 工作负载提供相对于按需型实例的折扣。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This option allow users to receive discounts by committing to use specific types of EC2 instances, suitable for long-running workloads. correct.

questions.domain: 領域 1：雲端概念 (Cloud Concepts)

question_text.zh: AWS 云的哪些特性可以帮助用户消除未充分利用的 CPU 容量？
question_text.en: Which characteristic of the AWS Cloud helps users eliminate underutilized CPU capacity?

A.zh: 敏捷
A.en: Agility

B.zh: 弹性
B.en: Elasticity

C.zh: 可靠性
C.en: Reliability

D.zh: 耐用性
D.en: Durability

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：性：敏捷性是指快速适应变化的能力，例如快速部署新的应用程序或服务。虽然敏捷性是 AWS 的重要属性，但它与消除未充分利用的 CPU 容量没有直接关系。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: refers to the ability to quickly adapt to changes, such as the rapid deployment of new applications or services. While agility is an important attribute of AWS, it is not directly related to eliminating underutilized CPU capacity.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：弹性 是AWS云的核心特性之一，让用户可以根据需求动态调整资源（例如CPU容量）。这直接帮助用户消除未充分利用的资源。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: This service is one of the core features of the AWS cloud, allowing users to dynamically adjust resources (such as CPU capacity) according to demand. This directly helps users eliminate underutilized resources.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：可靠性 是指系统在各种情况下维持正常运行的能力，与资源利用率没有直接关系。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: refers to the system's ability to maintain normal operation under various circumstances and has no direct relationship with resource utilization.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：持久性：持久性通常与数据存储相关，例如确保数据不会丢失。它与CPU容量的利用率没有直接关系，
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This service is often associated with data storage, such as ensuring that data is not lost. It is not directly related to the utilization of CPU capacity,

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：B (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：弹性 是AWS云的核心特性之一，让用户可以根据需求动态调整资源（例如CPU容量）。这直接帮助用户消除未充分利用的资源。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This service is one of the core features of the AWS cloud, allowing users to dynamically adjust resources (such as CPU capacity) according to demand. This directly helps users eliminate underutilized resources.

questions.domain: 領域 3：雲端技術與服務 (Cloud Technology and Services)

question_text.zh: 公司可以使用哪些 AWS 服务来实现松散耦合架构？ （选择两项。）
question_text.en: Which AWS services can a company use to achieve a loosely coupled architecture? (Choose two.)

A.zh: Amazon WorkSpaces
A.en: Amazon WorkSpaces

B.zh: Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS)
B.en: Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS)

C.zh: Amazon Connect
C.en: Amazon Connect

D.zh: AWS Trusted Advisor
D.en: AWS Trusted Advisor

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon WorkSpaces 是一项虚拟桌面服务，与松散耦合的架构如何，它都能提供远程工作解决方案无关。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This service is a virtual inspection service that provides remote working solutions regardless of loosely coupled architectures.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：Amazon SQS 是一种消息队列服务，可在分布式系统中实现异步通信，从而实现松散耦合的架构。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: Amazon SQS is a message queuing service that enables asynchronous communication in distributed systems, enabling loosely coupled architectures.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon Connect 是一项独立于松散耦合架构的客户支持云联系中心服务。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This service is a cloud contact center service for customer support that is independent of loosely coupled architectures.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Trusted Advisor 是一项优化建议服务，与松散耦合的架构如何，它都可以提供成本、安全性和其他建议无关。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This service is an optimization recommendation service that provides cost, security, and other recommendations regardless of loosely coupled architectures.

E.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS Step Functions 是一种工作流程编排服务，它通过定义状态机来协调多个分布式服务，从而实现松散耦合的架构。
E.en: Correct. Technical reason: This service is a workflow orchestration service that enables loosely coupled architectures by defining state machines to coordinate multiple distributed services.

answer_text.zh: B. Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS)；E. AWS Step Functions
answer_text.en: B. Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS); E. AWS Step Functions

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：BE (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：Amazon SQS 是一种消息队列服务，可在分布式系统中实现异步通信，从而实现松散耦合的架构。；AWS Step Functions 是一种工作流程编排服务，它通过定义状态机来协调多个分布式服务，从而实现松散耦合的架构。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: BE (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Amazon SQS is a message queuing service that enables asynchronous communication in distributed systems, enabling loosely coupled architectures.; This service is a workflow orchestration service that enables loosely coupled architectures by defining state machines to coordinate multiple distributed services.

questions.domain: 領域 4：計費、定價與支援 (Billing, Pricing, and Support)

question_text.zh: 如果超出自定义支出阈值，哪种 AWS 云服务可以向客户发送警报？
question_text.en: Which AWS Cloud service can send alerts to customers if custom spending thresholds are exceeded?

A.zh: AWS Budgets
A.en: AWS Budgets

B.zh: AWS Cost Explorer
B.en: AWS Cost Explorer

C.zh: AWS Cost Allocation Tags
C.en: AWS Cost Allocation Tags

D.zh: AWS Organizations
D.en: AWS Organizations

A.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS Budgets 允许消费者设置预算和支出阈值，并在超过阈值时发送警报。它是专门为成本管理和预算监控而设计的服务。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: This option allows consumers to set budget and spending thresholds and send alerts when thresholds are exceeded. It is a service specifically designed for cost management and budget monitoring.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Cost Explorer 主要用于分析和可视化成本和使用情况，但它不提供设置支出阈值警报的功能。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This service is primarily used to analyze and visualize costs and usage, but it does not provide the ability to set spend threshold alerts.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS 成本分配标签用于对资源进行标记和分类，以便更好地追踪成本，但它们不具有发送警报的功能。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: These choices are used to tag and classify resources to better track costs, but they do not have the functionality to send alerts.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Organizations 用于管理多个 AWS 账户的组织架构和权限，但不涉及预算或支出警报功能。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This service is used to manage the organizational structure and permissions of multiple AWS accounts, but it does not involve budgeting or spending alert features.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：A (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS Budgets 允许消费者设置预算和支出阈值，并在超过阈值时发送警报。它是专门为成本管理和预算监控而设计的服务。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This option allows consumers to set budget and spending thresholds and send alerts when thresholds are exceeded. It is a service specifically designed for cost management and budget monitoring.

questions.domain: 領域 1：雲端概念 (Cloud Concepts)

question_text.zh: 一家公司计划迁移到 AWS 云。该公司希望使用 AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) 来定义和追踪业务成果，作为其云转型之旅的一部分。哪一种 AWS CAF 治理视角功能可以满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company plans to migrate to the AWS Cloud. The company wants to use the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) to define and track business outcomes as part of its cloud transformation journey. Which AWS CAF governance perspective capability will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 福利管理
A.en: Benefits management

B.zh: 风险管理
B.en: Risk management

C.zh: 应用程序组合管理
C.en: Application portfolio management

D.zh: 云财务管理
D.en: Cloud financial management

A.zh: 正確。技术原因：（收入管理）收入管理是 AWS CAF 治理角度的核心功能，旨在帮助企业定义、追踪和实现业务成果。这完全符合题目中提到的「定义和追踪业务结果」。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: (Revenue Management) Revenue management is a core capability in the AWS CAF governance perspective, designed to help enterprises define, track, and achieve business results. This completely matches the "defining and tracking business results" mentioned in the title.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：（风险管理）风险管理是 AWS CAF 治理观点中的另一项功能，但它主要着重于识别和管理风险，而不是直接定义和追踪业务成果。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: (Risk Management) Risk management is another capability within the AWS CAF governance perspective, but it focuses primarily on identifying and managing risks rather than directly defining and tracking business outcomes.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：（应用程序组合管理）应用程序组合管理属于AWS CAF的平台视角，与治理视角无关，不涉及定义和追踪业务结果。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: (Application Portfolio Management) Application portfolio management belongs to the Platform Perspective of AWS CAF, has nothing to do with the governance perspective, and does not involve defining and tracking business results.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：（云财务管理）因此不符合题目要求。云财务管理是AWS CAF治理角度的能力之一，但它主要专注于成本优化和财务管理，而不是直接定义和追踪业务结果。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: (Cloud Financial Management) This option is wrong. Cloud financial management is one of the capabilities in the AWS CAF governance perspective, but it mainly focuses on cost optimization and financial management rather than directly defining and tracking business results.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：A (76%) C (18%) 6%。热门讨论与考点摘要：（收入管理）收入管理是 AWS CAF 治理角度的核心功能，旨在帮助企业定义、追踪和实现业务成果。这完全符合题目中提到的「定义和追踪业务结果」。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (76%) C (18%) 6%. Popular discussion and key point summary: (Revenue Management) Revenue management is a core capability in the AWS CAF governance perspective, designed to help enterprises define, track, and achieve business results. This completely matches the "defining and tracking business results" mentioned in the title.

questions.domain: 領域 3：雲端技術與服務 (Cloud Technology and Services)

question_text.zh: 公司需要在其客户端和 Amazon S3 存储桶之间快速、安全地远距离移动文件。哪个 S3 功能可以满足此要求？
question_text.en: A company needs to quickly and securely move files over long distances between its client and an Amazon S3 bucket. Which S3 feature will meet this requirement?

A.zh: S3 Versioning
A.en: S3 Versioning

B.zh: S3 Transfer Acceleration
B.en: S3 Transfer Acceleration

C.zh: S3 ACLs
C.en: S3 ACLs

D.zh: S3 Intelligent-Tiering
D.en: S3 Intelligent-Tiering

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：S3 版本控制用于管理多个对象样本，以协助防止意外删除或覆写数据。它与文件传输速度和安全性无关，
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This service is used to manage multiple specimens of objects to help prevent accidental deletion or overwriting of data. It has nothing to do with file transfer speed and security,

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：S3 传输加速通过使用 Amazon CloudFront 的全球分布式边缘网站加速文件上传，显著提高了跨区域传输的速度和安全性。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: significantly improves the speed and security of cross-region transfers by accelerating file uploads using Amazon CloudFront's globally distributed edge locations.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：S3ACL：S3 ACL（访问控制清单）用于管理对象的访问权限，与文件传输速度和安全性无关。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: S3ACLs: S3 ACLs (Access Control Lists) are used to manage access rights to objects and have nothing to do with file transfer speed and security.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：S3 智能分层是一种存储类，用于优化存储成本并根据访问模式自动将数据移动到不同的存储层。它与文件传输速度和安全性无关，
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This service is a storage class used to optimize storage costs and automatically move data to different storage tiers based on access patterns. It has nothing to do with file transfer speed and security,

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：B (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：S3 传输加速通过使用 Amazon CloudFront 的全球分布式边缘网站加速文件上传，显著提高了跨区域传输的速度和安全性。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: significantly improves the speed and security of cross-region transfers by accelerating file uploads using Amazon CloudFront's globally distributed edge locations.

questions.domain: 領域 4：計費、定價與支援 (Billing, Pricing, and Support)

question_text.zh: 一家公司需要在 Amazon EC2 实例上持续运行实验工作负载，并在 12 小时后停止实例。哪一种实例购买选项能够最经济高效地满足此要求？
question_text.en: A company needs to continuously run an experimental workload on an Amazon EC2 instance and stop the instance after 12 hours. Which instance purchasing option will meet this requirement MOST cost-effectively?

A.zh: 按需型实例
A.en: On-Demand Instances

B.zh: 预留实例
B.en: Reserved Instances

C.zh: 竞价型实例
C.en: Spot Instances

D.zh: Dedicated Instances
D.en: Dedicated Instances

A.zh: 正確。技术原因：按需型实例允许用户按需使用 EC2 运行实例，无需长期承诺或预付费用。非常适合短期、不可预测的工作负载，例如实验工作负载。虽然成本较高，但灵活性和无需提前规划使其成为 12 小时跑步的最佳选择。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: This option allow users to use EC2 execution instances on demand without long-term commitments or upfront costs. Ideal for short-term, unpredictable workloads such as experimental workloads. While the cost is higher, the flexibility and lack of advance planning make it the best option for 12-hour runs.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：预留实例要求用户事先承诺使用一定时间（1 年或 3 年）以换取折扣。不适合短期工作负载，因为它需要长期承诺并且缺乏灵活性。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: require users to commit to using them for a certain period of time (1 year or 3 years) in advance in exchange for discounts. Not suitable for short-term workloads as it requires a long-term commitment and lacks flexibility.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：竞价型实例价格便宜，但可能随时中断。对于需要连续运行12小时的工作负载，竞价型实例中断风险较高，不适合该场景。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: These choices are cheap but may be interrupted at any time. For workloads that need to run continuously for 12 hours, Spot Instances have a high risk of interruption and are not suitable for this scenario.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：专用实例提供物理隔离的服务器，价格更昂贵。通常用于满足合规性或特殊性能需求，不适合短期实验工作负载。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This option provide physically isolated servers, which are more expensive. Typically used to meet compliance or special performance needs and not suitable for short-term experimental workloads.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：A (78%) C (22%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：按需型实例允许用户按需使用 EC2 运行实例，无需长期承诺或预付费用。非常适合短期、不可预测的工作负载，例如实验工作负载。虽然成本较高，但灵活性和无需提前规划使其成为 12 小时跑步的最佳选择。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (78%) C (22%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This option allow users to use EC2 execution instances on demand without long-term commitments or upfront costs. Ideal for short-term, unpredictable workloads such as experimental workloads. While the cost is higher, the flexibility and lack of advance planning make it the best option for 12-hour runs.

questions.domain: 領域 1：雲端概念 (Cloud Concepts)

question_text.zh: AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) 的云转型之旅的哪个阶段着重于展示云如何协助加速实现业务成果？
question_text.en: Which cloud transformation journey phase of the AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (AWS CAF) focuses on demonstrating how the cloud helps accelerate business outcomes?

A.zh: 规模
A.en: Scale

B.zh: 设想
B.en: Envision

C.zh: 对齐
C.en: Align

D.zh: 启动
D.en: Launch

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：扩展名：错误。扩展阶段着重于扩展云使用量和优化云资源，而不是演示如何加速业务成果。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Extension: Error. The Scale phase focuses on scaling cloud usage and optimizing cloud resources, rather than demonstrating how to accelerate business results.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：概念：设想阶段的目标是展示云如何通过设想其潜力来帮助加速业务成果。这是 AWS CAF 中定义明确的阶段。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: Concept: Correct. The goal of the Envision phase is to demonstrate how the cloud can help accelerate business outcomes by envisioning its potential. This is a well-defined phase in AWS CAF.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：调整阶段的重点是确保云战略与业务目标保持一致，而不是直接展示加速的业务成果。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: ment: Error. The focus of the Align phase is to ensure that the cloud strategy is aligned with business goals, rather than directly demonstrating accelerated business results.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：启动阶段的目标是实际部署云解决方案，而不是演示云如何加速业务成果。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Startup: Error. The goal of the Launch phase is to actually deploy the cloud solution, not to demonstrate how the cloud accelerates business outcomes.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：B (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：概念：设想阶段的目标是展示云如何通过设想其潜力来帮助加速业务成果。这是 AWS CAF 中定义明确的阶段。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Concept: Correct. The goal of the Envision phase is to demonstrate how the cloud can help accelerate business outcomes by envisioning its potential. This is a well-defined phase in AWS CAF.

questions.domain: 領域 1：雲端概念 (Cloud Concepts)

question_text.zh: 在 AWS 责任共担模式下，哪个选项是客户责任？
question_text.en: Which option is a customer responsibility under the AWS shared responsibility model?

A.zh: Amazon EC2实例底层硬件维护
A.en: Maintenance of underlying hardware of Amazon EC2 instances

B.zh: 应用程序数据安全
B.en: Application data security

C.zh: 数据中心的物理安全
C.en: Physical security of data centers

D.zh: VPC组件维护
D.en: Maintenance of VPC components

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：维护底层硬件是 AWS 的责任，客户不负责维护底层硬件。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Maintaining the underlying hardware is the responsibility of AWS, and customers are not responsible for maintaining the underlying hardware.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：在责任共担模式下，客户对其应用程序的数据安全负责，包括加密、访问控制等。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: Under the shared responsibility model, customers are responsible for the data security of their applications, including encryption, access control, etc.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：数据中心的管理安全是 AWS 而不是客户的责任。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The management security of the data center is the responsibility of AWS and not the customer.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：VPC组件的底层维护由AWS 负责，客户只需配置和管理自己使用的资源即可。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The underlying maintenance of VPC components is the responsibility of AWS, and customers only need to configure and manage the resources they use.

answer_text.zh: B. 应用程序数据安全

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：B (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：在责任共担模式下，客户对其应用程序的数据安全负责，包括加密、访问控制等。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Under the shared responsibility model, customers are responsible for the data security of their applications, including encryption, access control, etc.

questions.domain: 領域 1：雲端概念 (Cloud Concepts)

question_text.zh: 一家公司希望其 Amazon EC2 实例能够在高度可用的环境中运行，即使特定地理区域发生自然灾害也是如此。哪种方法可以实现这个目标？
question_text.en: A company wants its Amazon EC2 instances to operate in a highly available environment, even if there is a natural disaster in a particular geographic area. Which approach will achieve this goal?

A.zh: 在多个 AWS 区域中使用 EC2 实例。
A.en: Use EC2 instances in multiple AWS Regions.

B.zh: 在多个 Amazon CloudFront 位置使用 EC2 实例。
B.en: Use EC2 instances in multiple Amazon CloudFront locations.

C.zh: 在多个边缘位置使用 EC2 实例。
C.en: Use EC2 instances in multiple edge locations.

D.zh: 在 AWS 本地扩展区中使用 EC2 实例。
D.en: Use EC2 instances in AWS Local Zones.

A.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS 区域是地理上分布的区域，旨在实现灾难恢复和高可靠性。如果一个Region发生天灾，其他Region仍可正常运行。这是实现高可用性和灾难恢复的最佳实践。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: AWS Regions are geographically distributed regions designed for disaster recovery and high reliability. If a natural disaster occurs in one Region, other Regions can still operate normally. This is a best practice for achieving high availability and disaster recovery.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：CloudFront 是一项内容交付网络 (CDN) 服务，旨在加速内容交付而不是运行 EC2 实例。 CloudFront 的边缘位置不适合部署在易于访问的资源位置。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: CloudFront is a content delivery network (CDN) service that is designed to accelerate content delivery rather than running EC2 instances. CloudFront's edge location is not suitable for deployment on highly accessible resource locations.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：边缘位置是 CloudFront 不可或缺的一部分，旨在存储内容和加速交付，而不是运行 EC2 实例。 Edge Locations 不具备 Region 的高可及性特征。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Edge Locations are an integral part of CloudFront and are designed to store content and accelerate delivery, rather than running EC2 instances. Edge Locations do not have the high accessibility feature of Region.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：本地区域是 AWS 提供的延迟优化区域。它们通常靠近当地资源，但依赖主要区域，在自然灾害中无法提供高可靠性。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Local Zones are latency optimization areas provided by AWS. They are usually located close to local resources, but they rely on the main Region and cannot provide high reliability in natural disasters.

answer_text.zh: A. 在多个 AWS 区域中使用 EC2 实例。
answer_text.en: A. Use EC2 instances in multiple AWS Regions.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：A (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS 区域是地理上分布的区域，旨在实现灾难恢复和高可靠性。如果一个Region发生天灾，其他Region仍可正常运行。这是实现高可用性和灾难恢复的最佳实践。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: AWS Regions are geographically distributed regions designed for disaster recovery and high reliability. If a natural disaster occurs in one Region, other Regions can still operate normally. This is a best practice for achieving high availability and disaster recovery.

questions.domain: 領域 1：雲端概念 (Cloud Concepts)

question_text.zh: 一家公司希望对单体应用程序进行现代化改造并将其转换为微服务。该公司希望将应用程序迁移到 AWS。公司应该使用哪种迁移策略？
question_text.en: A company wants to modernize and convert a monolithic application into microservices. The company wants to move the application to AWS. Which migration strategy should the company use?

A.zh: 重新主持
A.en: Rehost

B.zh: 平台重构
B.en: Replatform

C.zh: 回购
C.en: Repurchase

D.zh: 重构
D.en: Refactor

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Rehost（「提升和转移」）是将应用程序从本地环境直接迁移到云，无需对其进行任何修改。这种方式适合快速迁移，但不适合将单体应用转换为微服务。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: "lift and transfer") is the direct migration of an application from a local environment to the cloud without any modifications to it. This approach is suitable for quick migrations, but not suitable for converting monolithic applications into microservices.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Replatform 是对应用程序的轻微优化以利用云服务，但不会对应用程序架构进行重大更改。虽然它可以提高性能，但它并不涉及将单体应用程序转换为微服务。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This service is a slight optimization of the application to take advantage of cloud services, but does not make major changes to the application architecture. While it can improve performance, it doesn't involve converting a monolithic application into microservices.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：重新购买是指以新的解决方案取代现有应用程序，例如购买 SaaS 产品。这与题目中提到的「将单体应用程序转换为微服务」不一致。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This service is the replacement of an existing application with a new solution, such as the purchase of a SaaS product. This is inconsistent with "converting a monolithic application into a microservice" mentioned in the title.

D.zh: 正確。技术原因：重构 是对应用程序的重大架构更改，旨在优化其性能、可扩展性和可维护性。将单体应用转换为微服务是 Refactor 的典型场景。
D.en: Correct. Technical reason: This service is a major architectural change to an application to optimize its performance, scalability, and maintainability. Converting a monolithic application into microservices is a typical scenario of Refactor.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：D (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：重构 是对应用程序的重大架构更改，旨在优化其性能、可扩展性和可维护性。将单体应用转换为微服务是 Refactor 的典型场景。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This service is a major architectural change to an application to optimize its performance, scalability, and maintainability. Converting a monolithic application into microservices is a typical scenario of Refactor.

questions.domain: 領域 2：安全與合規 (Security and Compliance)

question_text.zh: 系统管理员为开发人员创建了一个新的 IAM 用户，并为该用户分配了访问密钥，而不是用户名和密码。访问密钥有什么用？
question_text.en: A systems administrator created a new IAM user for a developer and assigned the user an access key instead of a user name and password. What is the access key used for?

A.zh: 以 AWS 账户根用户身份访问 AWS 账户
A.en: To access the AWS account as the AWS account root user

B.zh: 通过 AWS 管理控制台访问 AWS 账户
B.en: To access the AWS account through the AWS Management Console

C.zh: 通过 CLI 访问 AWS 账户
C.en: To access the AWS account through a CLI

D.zh: 访问本公司的所有 AWS 账户
D.en: To access all of a company’s AWS accounts

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：通过 CLI 访问 AWS 账户错误：访问密钥是为 IAM 用户而不是根用户创建的。用户通常使用电子邮件和密码登录，而不是访问密码。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Access to AWS account via CLI error: The access key was created for the IAM user, not the root user. Users generally log in using email and a password, rather than accessing a password.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：无法使用访问密钥通过 AWS 管理控制台登录。登录管理控制台需要用户名和密码，而不是访问密钥。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Access to AWS account through AWS Management Console error: The access key cannot be used to log in through AWS Management Console. Login to the management console requires a username and password, not an access key.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：以 AWS 账户根用户身份访问 AWS 账户的访问密钥是用于通过 CLI、SDK 和 API 与 AWS 服务交互的凭证。它允许 IAM 用户以编程方式访问 AWS 资源。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: Accessing an AWS account as the AWS account root user is the access key is the credential used to interact with AWS services through the CLI, SDK, and API. It allows IAM users to programmatically access AWS resources.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：访问公司拥有的 AWS 账户错误：访问密钥仅与创建的 IAM 用户关联，并且仅在该用户所在的 AWS 账户内。它不能用于访问多个 AWS 账户。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Access to company-owned AWS account error: The access key is only associated with the IAM user created, and only within the AWS account in which that user exists. It cannot be used to access multiple AWS accounts.

answer_text.zh: C. 通过 CLI 访问 AWS 账户
answer_text.en: C. To access the AWS account through a CLI

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：以 AWS 账户根用户身份访问 AWS 账户的访问密钥是用于通过 CLI、SDK 和 API 与 AWS 服务交互的凭证。它允许 IAM 用户以编程方式访问 AWS 资源。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Accessing an AWS account as the AWS account root user is the access key is the credential used to interact with AWS services through the CLI, SDK, and API. It allows IAM users to programmatically access AWS resources.

questions.domain: 領域 1：雲端概念 (Cloud Concepts)

question_text.zh: 哪个选项是由一个或多个数据中心组成的环境？
question_text.en: Which option is an environment that consists of one or more data centers?

A.zh: Amazon CloudFront
A.en: Amazon CloudFront

B.zh: Availability Zone
B.en: Availability Zone

C.zh: 专有网络
C.en: VPC

D.zh: AWS Outposts
D.en: AWS Outposts

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon CloudFront 是一种内容传递网络 (CDN) 服务，用于将内容快速分发给世界各地的用户，而不是由数据中心组成的环境。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This service is a content delivery network (CDN) service used to quickly distribute content to users around the world, rather than an environment composed of data centers.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：可用区是 AWS 基础设施的一部分，由一个或多个数据中心组成，提供高可用性和容错能力。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: This service is part of the AWS infrastructure, consisting of one or more data centers, providing high availability and fault tolerance.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：VPC（Virtual Private Cloud）是用于在AWS上运行资源的虚拟网络环境，但它并未直接代表由数据中心组成的环境。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Virtual Private Cloud) is a virtual network environment used to run resources on AWS, but it does not directly represent an environment composed of data centers.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Outposts 是一种混合云解决方案，可将 AWS 服务扩展到本地数据中心，但它本身并不是由数据中心组成的环境。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This service is a hybrid cloud solution that extends AWS services to local data centers, but it is not an environment itself consisting of data centers.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：B (93%) 7%。热门讨论与考点摘要：可用区是 AWS 基础设施的一部分，由一个或多个数据中心组成，提供高可用性和容错能力。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (93%) 7%. Popular discussion and key point summary: This service is part of the AWS infrastructure, consisting of one or more data centers, providing high availability and fault tolerance.
